free_text: Light charged particle production in neutron-induced
reactions on aluminum at En=28.5-62.7 MeV
Benck, Slypen, Meulders
Corcalciuc
Chadwick, Young
Koning
Main reference
Final data tables for dA/dE, dA,dE,
sigma at the energy range 28.5 - 62.7 MeV
Description of data and
energy-angle integrated cross-sections at the energy
range 28.5 - 62.7 MeV
- prelim
angular distributions at 62.7 MeV
Experim. details
Exp.details
Present
data  compared with DDX of Silicon given in NSE,141,55
Data
for H-3, He-4 production on carbon and exp. details
Partial and
total kerma coefficients and integral cr.sect.for Al
Fast Neutron Facility Cyclotron
'Cyclone'
65 MeV proton beam strikes 0.3 mm Thick
natural lithium target. Proton current is 10 micro-A
Full width at half maximum is 2 MeV containing 50% of
neutrons,plus a flat continuum of low-energy neutrons.
-For energy calibration the proton and deuteron recoi-
ling from respectively a polypropylene (1 mm thick)
and deuterated polypropylene target (0.6 mm thick)
Were Used.
Four charged particles detector
telescopes were used simultaneously. Each of them
consists of
(I) 'Delta-E' Detector NE102 plastic scintillator,
0.1 mm thick, 4 cm diameter, and
(II) Cs-I(Tl) 'E' detector, crystal 22 mm thick,
38.1 mm diameter
The Time-Of-Flight method was deduced from the
time difference between the capacitive beam pick off
located upstream from the neutron-producing target,
and the Delta-E detector. Only those events of
charged particles detection  were registered which
are associated with neutrons in the main neutron
peak.
Metallic Al target 1 mm thick and 5*5 cm-sq surface
Multistep Direct Models of Feshbach-Kerman-
Koonin, distorted wave Born approximation and
statistical description of excited states
Total error includes stat. error and
systematic uncertainties.
Systematic uncertainty includes:
Beam monitoring error
Statistics of the recoil protons error
Solid angle correction error
Error of the standard H(n,p) cross section
Normalization factors of the order of 3-4 x 10**(-4)
mb/MeV/sr were obtained, indicative of the detection
sensibility of the experiment.
Error of number of target nuclei, etc.
* EN-RSL - half width of the incident neuron energy bin
Data obtained via  Internet and checked with
Sylvie Benck, 2000-11-10.
+ +  Compiled by S.M.
. .  References added     .
. .  17 New Subentries Added
Last checking has been done.

Subentries 5, 9, 13, 17, 21 and 23
Corrected by SM, SAN 004, 008, 012, 016,020

were moved to separated REACTION (multiple reaction
formalism used) in Subents 002, 004, 006, 008, 010,
012, 014, 016. Subents 018 - 025 deleted.Information
from Subents 018,020,022,024 already presented in other
are calculated. Ref. PMB,44,(1),1,1999 corrected on
PMB,45,29,2000 and moved to REL-REF.  ERR-ANALYS update
All values=0. without error are deleted following Tbl.
of AND,78,161,2001 and confirmation from Dr.S.Benck.
